stem: A manufacturing plant produces widgets at a rate of 100 widgets per hour during regular operation. Due to equipment wear, 10% of widgets produced are defective and must be discarded. The plant needs to deliver 1,800 non-defective widgets. The plant can operate for up to 8 hours at the regular rate, but any additional production requires overtime at a rate of 80 widgets per hour (still with 10% defect rate). How many total hours of operation (regular plus overtime) are needed to produce exactly 1,800 non-defective widgets?
explanation:
Non-defective rate = 90% = 0.90
Regular production: 100 widgets/hr × 0.90 = 90 non-defective widgets/hr
Overtime production: 80 widgets/hr × 0.90 = 72 non-defective widgets/hr

After 8 hours regular: 8 × 90 = 720 non-defective widgets
Still needed: 1,800 - 720 = 1,080 non-defective widgets

Overtime hours needed: 1,080 ÷ 72 = 15 hours

Total hours: 8 + 15 = 23 hours

distractorRationale.A: Ignored both the defect rate and overtime rate change, dividing total by the regular production rate: 1800 ÷ 100 = 18
distractorRationale.B: Used the regular non-defective rate of 90/hr for overtime instead of the overtime non-defective rate of 72/hr: 8 regular hours produce 720, remaining 1800 - 720 = 1080, then 1080 ÷ 90 = 12, total = 8 + 12 = 20
distractorRationale.C: Correct: 8 hours regular (720 non-defective) + 15 hours overtime (1080 non-defective at 72/hr) = 23 hours
distractorRationale.D: Divided total non-defective widgets needed by the overtime non-defective rate alone, ignoring the 8-hour regular period: 1800 ÷ 72 = 25
